mission_anchor: Fully implement `pi-code-index` across all explicitly documented project phases available in the repository, starting from the normative Phase 1 MVP and continuing through any additional concrete phase specs, while preserving the documented architecture of a thin pi extension backed by a local daemon.
plan_basis: Repo truth plus `AGENTS.md` and `docs/mvp-spec.md`: Phase 1 MVP is the only normative implementation phase currently present. `docs/rfc.md` rollout phases remain rationale-only until promoted by a later explicit phase doc.

candidate_slices.goal: Replace the scaffolded `/index` placeholders with a thin extension-to-daemon control plane over a Unix domain socket, including daemon lazy-start and handshake, git-repo eligibility checks, repo registration keyed by repo root and worktree identity, and truthful `/index`, `/index enable`, `/index status`, and `/index doctor` flows backed by real baseline/overlay storage anchors.
acceptance_criteria: The extension-backed `/index` command no longer emits scaffold placeholders: `/index` shows current-repo status, `/index enable` refuses outside git repos, lazy-starts the daemon on supported POSIX hosts, performs a protocol handshake over a Unix domain socket, and registers the repo runtime by canonical repo root plus worktree identity.

The daemon behaves as a same-user singleton with Unix domain socket transport under a user-private cache directory and exposes at least `health`, `openRepo`, `enableRepoIndexing`, `getStatus`, and `getRepoDiagnostics` through the real client/server boundary; protocol mismatch is surfaced as an explicit error instead of a placeholder message.

Repo registration creates durable SQLite-backed storage anchors for the current HEAD baseline and current worktree overlay, with schema/indexer metadata and predictable paths consistent with the one-baseline-per-commit and one-overlay-per-worktree model, and `/index status` plus `/index doctor` report truthful repo state, transport, protocol, repo root, worktree ID, and actionable errors.
Deterministic checks cover daemon lazy-start and handshake, non-git refusal, and the `/index enable` -> `/index status` -> `/index doctor` control-plane flow.
candidate_slices.why_now: The repository is still a scaffold. Without a real daemon, IPC boundary, repo identity model, and truthful command flow, no later Phase 1 indexing or tool slice can be implemented or verified credibly.
evidence: `/index` now routes through `src/extension/commands/index-command.ts` and `src/extension/daemon-client.ts`, where canonical git repo resolution, daemon lazy-start, protocol handshake, and truthful status/doctor output replace scaffold placeholders.

`src/daemon/server.ts` now exposes real Unix-domain-socket methods `health`, `openRepo`, `enableRepoIndexing`, `disableRepoIndexing`, `getStatus`, `getRepoDiagnostics`, and `reindexRepo` behind a same-user singleton daemon rooted in a user-private cache directory.

`src/daemon/sqlite-store.ts` now creates durable baseline and overlay SQLite anchors under `~/.cache/pi-index/repos/<repoId>/...` with `schemaVersion`, `indexerVersion`, `languageAdapterSet`, and `createdAt` metadata, while `src/daemon/repo-runtime.ts` surfaces truthful transport, repo, and storage diagnostics.
Commit 329dab218489e7abf9ad579f006eb671d032a6b3 passed deterministic verification via `npm run check`, `npm test`, and the refreshed `.agent/verify_completion_stop.sh` baseline verifier.

`completion-reviewer` recorded pass / acceptance with no findings in `.agent/current/slice-history.jsonl`, so `P1-CTRL-DAEMON-SPINE` is canonically accepted at HEAD 329dab218489e7abf9ad579f006eb671d032a6b3.
locked_notes: Treat `docs/mvp-spec.md` as the sole normative Phase 1 source; RFC rollout phases are rationale only unless promoted elsewhere.
Keep the extension thin; heavy lifecycle, IPC, storage, and repo state belong in the daemon.
Preserve the existing `.gitignore` `.agent/` ignore housekeeping delta during implementation.

candidate_slices.goal: Implement the Phase 1 indexing core: git-aware discovery and safety filtering, transactional SQLite baseline plus overlay lifecycle, TS/JS structural fact extraction, coarse non-primary fallback summaries, and truthful `/index disable` plus `/index reindex` state transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Git-aware file discovery is bounded to the canonical repo root and finds eligible files via tracked files, modified tracked files, and untracked non-ignored files only, while enforcing the Phase 1 hard safety boundaries, default excludes, sensitive-file denylist, binary detection, and 2 MiB size cap.

The daemon maintains one immutable baseline DB per commit and one mutable overlay DB per worktree, commits query-visible writes transactionally, and answers status or doctor with truthful coverage, pending-file, and storage metadata.

TS/JS files receive structural symbol, import, export, reference, and file-summary extraction with `analysisQuality: structural`, while non-primary languages receive coarse file-summary fallback with `analysisQuality: basic` only.

`/index disable` stops repo background activity without purging cache by default, `/index reindex` performs a soft rebuild, and deterministic tests exercise `initializing`, `indexing`, `ready`, `stale`, `error`, and `disabled` transitions.
candidate_slices.why_now: A real control plane and repo registration path are prerequisite for truthful indexing, storage, and lifecycle semantics.
evidence: `src/daemon/server.ts` now performs git-bounded baseline and overlay discovery, enforces Phase 1 excludes/denylist/binary/2 MiB safety gates, schedules soft rebuild jobs, and surfaces truthful `initializing`, `indexing`, `ready`, `stale`, `error`, and `disabled` lifecycle states.

`src/daemon/sqlite-store.ts` now persists transactional baseline and overlay file facts, omitted-file records, structural symbol/import/export/reference rows, and coverage metadata in durable SQLite tables rooted under the existing repo anchors.
`src/daemon/tsjs-analyzer.ts` now provides dependency-free TS/JS structural extraction with `analysisQuality: structural`, while non-primary files are persisted through fallback basic summaries only.

`tests/index-storage.test.ts` deterministically covers discovery/filtering, immutable baseline plus mutable overlay population, structural vs basic analysis quality, and `/index disable` / `/index reindex` / `stale` / `error` transitions; `tests/control-plane.test.ts` was updated for truthful asynchronous readiness semantics.
Commit a18d882752cc0f0066181ae2fd4b84e8ca79da0f passed deterministic verification via `npm run check` and `npm test`; current HEAD preserves the same product diff plus canonical workflow bookkeeping.
`completion-reviewer` recorded pass / acceptance with no findings for the same slice at current HEAD d47dac0aea61391d9d75650839bf632d55599a49, so `P1-INDEX-STORAGE-PIPELINE` is canonically accepted.
locked_notes: One immutable baseline DB per commit plus one mutable overlay DB per worktree is mandatory in Phase 1.
TS/JS are the only primary structural analyzers; non-primary languages must stay coarse/fallback-only in MVP.

candidate_slices.goal: Expose and wire the approved Phase 1 query tools with deterministic results, freshness and coverage metadata, and shipped docs plus test parity for the local-only extension and daemon architecture.
acceptance_criteria: The extension registers only the approved Phase 1 tools `symbol_lookup`, `file_summary`, and `impact_analysis`, and activates them only when cwd is inside an enabled, healthy git repo.

Daemon query methods `symbolLookup`, `fileSummary`, and `impactAnalysis` return the required Phase 1 fields, deterministic ordering, documented caps and truncation metadata, plus `freshness`, `coverage`, `provenance`, and `analysisQuality` metadata.

TS/JS structural results and fallback basic results are both surfaced through the tool APIs without exposing RFC-only tools or advanced retrieval features such as `smart_read`, `codebase_search`, embeddings, or shared-baseline behavior.

README and deterministic tests describe and verify the shipped command and tool behavior, local-only daemon architecture, and operator recovery path through `/index doctor`, `/index reindex`, and `/index disable`.
candidate_slices.why_now: The tool surface and shipped docs depend on the earlier slices producing real indexed data, operational repo states, and a truthful daemon boundary.
evidence: `src/shared/protocol.ts`, `src/extension/daemon-client.ts`, and `src/daemon/server.ts` now expose only the approved Phase 1 query methods `symbolLookup`, `fileSummary`, and `impactAnalysis` with deterministic caps, truncation metadata, freshness, coverage, provenance, and analysis-quality signaling.

`src/extension/index.ts` now registers only `symbol_lookup`, `file_summary`, and `impact_analysis`, and gates their activation to enabled healthy Git repos via daemon-backed status checks before agent start.

`tests/tools-parity.test.ts` and `tests/check.ts` deterministically verify tool registration, activation gating, structural and fallback query surfacing, documented caps, and operator-facing formatting; `README.md` now documents the shipped local-only daemon architecture and `/index doctor` / `/index reindex` / `/index disable` recovery path.
Commit b7fe5c9eaa8d8194011555960e7a86b1743526f5 passed deterministic verification via `npm run check` and `npm test`.
locked_notes: Do not add `smart_read`, `codebase_search`, embeddings, shared-baseline reuse, or other RFC-only post-MVP features.
